steps.title: Move from claim to example
steps.content: The first sentence gives a general claim: Henrietta Lewis successfully challenged discrimination in California. The next sentence gives one concrete instance of that claim, her 1866 lawsuit against a streetcar company.

steps.title: Reject wrong transitions
steps.content:
• Choice A signals a result, but the lawsuit is an example of the claim, not a consequence of it.
  • Choice B signals simple chronology, which misses the example relationship.
  • Choice C signals an additional separate point, not a specific illustration.
The remaining choice introduces a specific example. The answer is D.
